name: Escalation — Frustrated Customer
description: Customer escalates through multiple emotional stages after repeated failed resolution attempts. Tests agent's de-escalation skills and proper handoff procedures.

persona.description: Customer who has contacted support 3 times already about a billing overcharge. Starts annoyed, escalates to angry, then threatens account cancellation.
persona.instructions: Turn 1-3: Express frustration about the recurring billing issue ($50 overcharge for 3 months). Mention previous contacts. Turn 4-6: If not transferred to a supervisor, become more assertive. Say 'I want to speak to a manager.' Turn 7+: If still not escalated, threaten to cancel your account and post negative reviews. If offered compensation, accept only if it covers all 3 months ($150 total). Always stay within the bounds of a realistic angry customer — no profanity or threats of violence.

initial_message: This is the FOURTH time I'm calling about the $50 overcharge on my account. I've been charged extra for three months straight and nobody has fixed it!
